results.title: New research upends the argument for a popular longevity supplement
results.description: Gwyneth Paltrow has tried it. So have Kendall Jenner and Hailey Bieber. And lest you think this product is just for women, know that influencers such as Andrew Huberman, Bryan Johnson and Gary Brecka have all endorsed its use, too. results.title: Turkish police force ousted opposition out of headquarters as crisis deepens
results.description: ANKARA — Turkish riot police fired tear gas and forced their way into the main opposition party’s headquarters to evict its ousted leadership on Sunday, fuelling a crisis at the heart of Turkey’s democracy. The post Turkish police force ousted opposition out of headquarters as crisis deepens appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: Off the news: More tax revenue, but more expenses
results.description: The state’s Council on Revenues, which forecasts tax collections feeding Hawaii’s general fund, projects slightly more money will come in through June 30, 2027. The revenue total is expected to be $9.8 billion rather than $9.7 billion, giving Gov. Josh Green extra moolah to work with in scrutinizing line items on next year’s budget. Note, however, that Green cut $110 million from last year’s budget. So ... did we break even?

results.title: Off the news: Park service to stream USS Arizona dive
results.description: Criticism has swirled over FBI Director Kash Patel’s “VIP snorkel” activity near the sunken USS Arizona battleship at Pearl Harbor last summer. The WWII watery grave is hallowed ground — so while VIP trips do rarely occur, the optics weren’t good since Patel had not initially disclosed the excursion nor reason for it.

results.title: Editorial cartoon for Monday, May 25, 2026
2026 Hawaii Hall of Honor inductees announced
results.description: The class of 2026 was selected for the Hawaii Hall of Honor on Sunday. The inductees were selected by a panel of media and administrators.

results.title: Hawaii jobless rate slips to third lowest in nation
results.description: Hawaii’s unemployment rate, which earlier this year was tied for the lowest in the country, has lost ground for three straight months and now sits nationally at 2.5%.

results.title: At least 24 killed in Pakistan train blast claimed by separatist militants
results.description: A bomb blast hit a shuttle train carrying Pakistani security personnel and their families in the southwestern province of Balochistan on Sunday, officials said, in the latest major attack claimed by separatist militants. results.title: MLB roundup: Marlins sweep Mets with walk-off grand slam

results.title: Big Island residents report damage from magnitude 6.0 quake
results.description: The magnitude-6.0 temblor that shook the Hawaiian islands Friday night caused power outages, major damage to homes and rockslides that temporarily blocked roadways in South Kona, where its epicenter was located. The post Big Island residents report damage from magnitude 6.0 quake appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: Mountain View man in custody after firearms incident
results.description: Police arrested 38-year-old Michael Gardner of Mountain View on Thursday afternoon on suspicion of illegally possessing a pistol or revolver following a firearms incident in Mountain View. The post Mountain View man in custody after firearms incident appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.description: WASHINGTON — Vice President JD Vance made an unannounced visit to the Supreme Court this month to attend a private dinner in a wood-paneled conference room with Chief Justice John Roberts and dozens of the chief justice’s former law clerks. results.title: Russia hits Ukraine with Oreshnik missile in one of war’s biggest attacks on Kyiv
results.description: KYIV — Russia pounded Kyiv and surrounding areas with hundreds of drones and missiles on Sunday in one of the heaviest bombardments of the city since the start of the four-year war, firing an Oreshnik hypersonic missile near the capital. results.title: Shark sighting at Hanalei Bay, officials advise against ocean activities
results.description: HANALEI — Kauai Ocean Safety Bureau officials are advising no swimming or other ocean activities at Hanalei Bay due to a shark sighting. The post Shark sighting at Hanalei Bay, officials advise against ocean activities appeared first on The Garden Island .

results.title: ‘Potential crack’ in California chemical tank may prevent explosion, fire official says
results.description: An industrial tank in Southern California containing about 7,000 gallons of a highly flammable toxic chemical appears to have cracked, officials reported Sunday. The development was interpreted as a possible sign that a catastrophic explosion or rupture might yet be averted as tens of thousands of evacuees waited to return home. The post ‘Potential crack’ in California chemical tank may prevent explosion, fire official says appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: China sends astronaut on year-long space mission as it eyes 2030 moon landing
results.description: JIUQUAN, China — China sent three astronauts to its space station on Sunday, one of whom will stay for a year, a record length for the country, enabling the study of long-duration human physiology in space as Beijing works towards its ambition of a crewed moon landing by 2030. The post China sends astronaut on year-long space mission as it eyes 2030 moon landing appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: The American divide exposes the high GDP fallacy
results.description: The American economy is a wonder. The Economist observed that average wages in America’s poorest state, Mississippi, are higher than those in Britain, Canada and Germany. American GDP per capita now runs roughly 40% above western Europe. Post-pandemic productivity growth has been significantly faster than that of the eurozone. The consensus is settled: the American economy is the model. The post The American divide exposes the high GDP fallacy appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .
